# devhost

> Per-directory port virtualization for dev servers on macOS and Linux. Every
> project — and every git worktree — gets its own deterministic loopback IP
> (hashed from the project root path into 127.77.0.0/16), so any number of
> projects can bind the same port (e.g. :3000) simultaneously. No port
> remapping, no dev servers killing each other, no application code changes.

devhost is a single static Go binary (stdlib-only, MIT). A project opts in by
committing an empty `.devhost` marker file; git worktrees inherit it. A PATH
shim loads a bind() interposer at the final exec, transparently rebinding any
dynamically-linked runtime (Node, Python, Ruby, JVM; Go too on macOS) to the
project IP with no app config. `<dirname>.devhost` hostnames resolve to it,
and an optional daemon routes `localhost:<port>` by the calling process's
working directory — so `curl localhost:3000` (or a Playwright-launched
browser) inside a worktree reaches that worktree's server. Built for parallel
AI coding agents running one dev server per worktree.

Install (pick one):
- `curl -fsSL https://wickedev.github.io/devhost/install.sh | sh`
- `brew install wickedev/tap/devhost`
- `go install github.com/wickedev/devhost/cmd/devhost@latest`

Then once per machine: `devhost setup`. Once per project: `devhost init`
(commit the `.devhost` marker). Other commands: `ip`, `name`, `exec`, `ls`,
`daemon`, `doctor`.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/wickedev/devhost/blob/main/README.md): full
  usage, platform notes, runtime coverage, roadmap
- [Architecture](https://github.com/wickedev/devhost/blob/main/docs/architecture.md):
  the tiered rebinding strategy, why DYLD interposition fails on macOS (SIP),
  the localhost mirror-router design, the privilege model, and the planned
  Linux eBPF backend

## Source

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/wickedev/devhost): Go source, MIT
- [Releases](https://github.com/wickedev/devhost/releases): prebuilt
  binaries for darwin/linux, amd64/arm64
- [Homebrew tap](https://github.com/wickedev/homebrew-tap): formula source
